creator: tz@freebsd.org
text:
Aloha Rainer,

the update of PHP 7.0 was done by joneum. Since i will be away for the next 3 to 5 months he will keep PHP up to date.

As war as i can see from the commit-logs he just did not remember to set the MFH. Therefore i assign this PR to him, so he can take care of it.

Some background information for you: MFH right is not bound to the maintainer. Often the maintainer is not even a committer and can't commit the update. When committet, an MFH request must be send to portmgr. Than portmgr will check if the MFH is fine and will approve it. When approved, we can commit the MFH. If not, than not. Often portmgr needs some time, because they are very careful to not destroy thinks. But in this case they did not get any notifications.

So, Jochen: can you please MFH your commits 475028 and 475029?

Thanks and Greetings,
Torsten
